title: KelpDAO/LayerZero DeFi contagion risk
asset_or_sector: DeFi / Liquid Restaking Tokens (rsETH) / Lending Protocols (Aave)
what_changed: On April 18, 2026, attackers exploited KelpDAO's LayerZero OFT bridge via an RPC poisoning attack, draining 116,500 rsETH, valued at roughly $290 million. The stolen tokens were used as collateral on Aave to borrow $236 million in ETH. The source brief says DeFi TVL fell by $13 billion within 48 hours and Aave froze rsETH and WETH markets while major stablecoin markets hit 100% utilization.
why_it_matters: The source brief frames the exploit as evidence of systemic risk in single-verifier cross-chain infrastructure and heavily leveraged liquid restaking token collateral, with possible bad debt fallout and frozen lending liquidity.
catalyst: KelpDAO's 1-of-1 LayerZero DVN configuration plus Aave's high loan-to-value allowance for rsETH collateral.
risks: Potential bad debt realization on Aave could deepen the fallout.
Prolonged freezing of major lending markets could suppress DeFi activity and liquidity.
invalidation_conditions: Aave successfully socializes or isolates the bad debt without major user losses.
Liquidity returns to normal utilization levels.
boundary: No trading execution, buy/sell instruction, or portfolio mutation. Research and monitoring only.
